steps.title: Distribute and combine like terms
steps.content: Distribute the negative and combine like terms:$$=7x^3+7x-6x^3+3x$$$$=x^3+10x$$

steps.title: Match to the answer choices
steps.content: The simplified expression is $x^3+10x$. That matches choice A — A is correct.

steps.title: Use the source rationale
steps.content: Choice A is correct. Applying the distributive property, the given expression can be written as $7{x}^{3}+7x-6{x}^{3}+3x$. Grouping like terms in this expression yields $\left(7{x}^{3}-6{x}^{3}\right)+\left(7x+3x\right)$. Combining like terms in this expression yields ${x}^{3}+10x$.

steps.title: Check the choices
steps.content: Choice B is incorrect and may result from conceptual or calculation errors.
